meta.index_title: CumulusVPN — Decentralized VPN on Flux Cloud
meta.index_desc: One-tap private VPN on the Flux network. No accounts, no logs, no email. Free forever at 100 KB/s — full speed for $0.99/month, paid in FLUX from your own wallet.
meta.index_social_title: CumulusVPN — Private internet, no account, no logs.
meta.index_social_desc: Decentralized VPN on Flux Cloud. Powered by RunOnFlux. Free at 100 KB/s, full speed for $0.99/month in FLUX.
meta.jsonld_offer_free: Free forever at 100 KB/s. No account, no email.
meta.jsonld_offer_premium: Full speed, per month, paid in FLUX from your own wallet.
meta.support_desc: CumulusVPN support — answers to common questions about connecting, privacy, premium, and troubleshooting. No account needed; email us if you're stuck.
meta.support_og_desc: Common questions about connecting, privacy, premium and troubleshooting — plus how to reach us.
meta.privacy_desc: CumulusVPN privacy policy — no account, no logs, no email. Exactly what we do and do not collect, in plain language.
meta.privacy_og_desc: No account, no logs, no email. What CumulusVPN does and does not collect, in plain language.

shared.footer_disclaimer_index: Decentralized VPN on Flux Cloud · cumulusvpn.com. CumulusVPN is an independent project built on Flux infrastructure; not affiliated with the makers of WireGuard. WireGuard is a registered trademark of Jason A. Donenfeld. FLUX price shown (~$0.99) is approximate and moves with the market. Availability may be restricted where VPNs are unlawful. POC preview — figures illustrative pending mainnet launch.
shared.footer_disclaimer_docs:
Decentralized VPN on Flux Cloud · cumulusvpn.com. CumulusVPN is an independent project built on
        Flux infrastructure; not affiliated with the makers of WireGuard. WireGuard is a registered
        trademark of Jason A. Donenfeld.

index.hero_h1:
Private internet,
no account, no logs.
index.hero_lede:
One tap connects you through datacenter nodes in dozens of countries.
        Free at 100 KB/s forever. Full speed for $0.99/month — paid in FLUX, straight from
        your wallet. No email, no card, no company that can hand over what it never had.
index.how_h2: No accounts. No server of ours. Just the chain.
index.how_lede:
Your WireGuard key is your identity. Payment is a fact on the Flux blockchain that every
        gateway reads independently. If our company vanished tomorrow, the deployed gateways keep running.
index.step1_p:
The app makes a WireGuard keypair on your device and enrolls it at the nearest datacenter
          gateway. Free tier is throttled to 100 KB/s server-side — connected in seconds, no signup.
index.step2_p:
Send 20 FLUX (~$0.99) to the payment address with your key's code in the transaction
          message. One tap opens your wallet with it pre-filled.
index.step3_p:
Every gateway scans the chain and flips your key to full speed within ~1 minute — on all
          servers at once, for 30 days. No activation, no login. Prepay to stack months.
index.pricing_h2: Free that actually works. Cheap when you need more.
index.pricing_lede:
The free tier is funded by subscribers and capped so it can't be abused — genuinely usable
        for browsing and messaging. Full speed is one wallet transfer away.
index.free_li3: No logs, no email, no signup
index.prem_li1: Full speed — up to 50 Mbit/s per device
index.prem_li2: Unlocks on all gateways at once
index.prem_li3: Pay from any FLUX wallet, prepay months
index.prem_li4: Still no account — no logs, no email, nothing stored
index.apps_h2: One connect button, on every screen you own.
index.apps_lede:
The same beautiful client on web, desktop and mobile — sharing one design language and one
        key. Pay on web or desktop; your phone unlocks automatically over the chain within a minute.
index.app_web_p:
Generates your key in the browser and gives you a WireGuard config + QR to import into the
          WireGuard app. Also hosts the pay-in-FLUX upgrade page. No install to set up or pay.
index.app_desktop_p:
A calm menu-bar app for macOS, Windows and Linux. Kill switch, auto-reconnect and gateway
          failover run quietly in the tray. Signed ~10 MB installers.
index.app_mobile_p:
iOS and Android, connect-only and store-compliant. Upgrade on the web and your phone flips
          to full speed on its own — no in-app billing, no lock-in.
index.trust_h2: We can't hand over what we never had.
index.trust_lede:
Most VPNs ask you to trust a no-logs promise. CumulusVPN removes the thing you'd have to
        trust: there's no account to log, no database of users, and no central server that owns the fleet.
index.trust1_p:
Gateways enforce tier per key and route traffic — nothing about who or what you browse is
          stored. DNS runs through the tunnel to the gateway's own resolver.
index.trust2_p:
No email, no password, no card. Your WireGuard key is your only identity, and payment is
          an anonymous FLUX transfer tied to a hash — not to you.
index.trust3_p:
The apps and the gateway are public. Read the code, build it yourself, or run your own —
          the payment and entitlement protocol is fully documented.
index.trust4_p:
Exits run on Flux datacenter infrastructure — not a residential botnet. Stable IPs, real
          bandwidth, over 6,500 nodes across the network.
index.trust5_p:
Clients discover live gateways from the Flux network itself, with a signed bundled
          fallback. No single point to seize, block, or switch off.
index.trust6_p:
Modern, audited, fast. You never touch a config file — the app handles keys, tunnels and
          failover. WireGuard is the wire; the experience is one button.
index.band_p:
Start free in seconds — no email, no card. Upgrade with one FLUX transfer whenever you want
        full speed, and your $0.99 literally buys the network's own compute.

support.intro_p: No account needed — most answers are right here. There's no login and no password, so there's nothing to recover. Still stuck? Email us.
support.a1:
A decentralized WireGuard VPN that runs on the Flux cloud. One tap routes
          your traffic through datacenter nodes worldwide. No account, no logs.
support.a2:
No. On first launch the app generates a WireGuard key on your device —
          that key is your only identity. No email, password, or phone number, ever.
support.a3:
Open the app, pick a country, and tap connect. On the first connection your
          phone shows a standard operating-system VPN-permission prompt — approve it once.
support.q4: Is the free tier really free?
support.a4:
Yes, forever. It's capped at 100 KB/s (fine for browsing and chat), with
          no card and no trial.
support.a5:
Every gateway in the fleet — 30+ countries at launch — and the list updates
          live from the network.
support.a6: No activity logs. Gateways hold minimal routing state in RAM only, erased on disconnect or restart. Your browsing, DNS queries, and traffic are never recorded. See the privacy policy for the full detail.
support.a7:
A WireGuard keypair generated on your device. The private key never leaves
          your phone; only the public key is shared, to route your tunnel. It's a pseudonym, not tied to
          your identity.
support.q8: What can a gateway operator see?
support.a8:
Like any VPN exit, real-time encrypted flow metadata — but no log we create,
          and never your traffic contents. Turn on multi-hop so no single server sees both who you are and
          where you're going.
support.a9:
It routes your traffic through two gateways, so no single server sees both
          your IP and your destination. It's slower — the app tells you how much — and it's off by
          default.
support.a10:
No. CumulusVPN protects you from local-network snooping, ISP logging, and
          geo-restrictions, but it is not anonymity software like Tor. We say so plainly.
support.q11: How do I upgrade to premium?
support.a11: Premium (full speed) is purchased on the web at vpn.cumulusvpn.com with FLUX from your own wallet. The mobile app has no purchase screen — your device unlocks on its own about a minute after payment confirms.
support.q12: What is FLUX and how do I get it?
support.a12:
FLUX is the cryptocurrency of the Flux network. Buy it on an exchange and hold
          it in a wallet such as Zelcore or SSP, then pay from there.
support.a13:
Your entitlement is computed from the public blockchain and keyed to your
          public key, so every gateway flips your key to full speed within about a minute — on all servers
          at once, for 30 days.
support.a14:
Yes. Send a multiple of the monthly amount and the extra months stack (up to
          24).
support.a15:
No. Payment is FLUX from your own wallet, on the web — no card, no billing
          account, nothing stored about you.
support.q16: The app shows "no gateways" or no countries.
support.a16:
Your device probably has no working internet, or a captive portal
          (hotel/airport Wi-Fi) is blocking the gateway probe. Reconnect and reopen the app — discovery
          retries automatically.
support.a17:
Approve the operating-system VPN-permission prompt on the first connect. If
          another VPN is already active, disconnect it first, then try again — or pick a different
          country.
support.a18:
The free tier is capped at 100 KB/s server-side. Upgrade to premium for
          full speed.
support.q19: What is the VPN permission prompt?
support.a19:
Android and iOS require your explicit consent before any app can create a VPN
          tunnel. It's the operating system asking, once — approve it to connect.
support.q20: How do I fully remove myself?
support.a20:
Disconnect, then regenerate or delete your key in the app's settings. That
          severs the pseudonymous link; gateway state is RAM-only and already clears when you
          disconnect.
support.ch_email_p: Support, plus privacy and data requests. We answer from a real inbox.
support.ch_github_p: Report a bug or request a feature — the app and gateway are open source.
support.ch_community_p: Discord / Telegram community for tips and announcements.
support.ch_social_p: Follow along on X for release notes and status.

privacy.lead: CumulusVPN is a decentralized VPN service. This policy explains, in plain language, exactly what we do and do not collect. It is short because the service is built to collect almost nothing. If a claim here is ever wrong, that is a bug we will fix — the architecture is described publicly at cumulusvpn.com and in our source code.
privacy.contact_line:
Contact for privacy questions and data requests: info@cumulusvpn.com
Abuse reports: info@cumulusvpn.com
privacy.s1_h: 1. Summary (the whole policy in one screen)
privacy.s1_li1: No account. No email. No password. No phone number. You never give us your identity.
privacy.s1_li2: No activity logs. We do not record the websites you visit, your DNS queries, your traffic contents, your bandwidth history, or connection timestamps to any persistent store.
privacy.s1_li3: Your identity is a cryptographic key, generated on your own device. We never see your private key. We only ever see a public key, which is a random-looking string with no link to you.
privacy.s1_li4: Gateways keep peer state in RAM only. When a server restarts, that state is gone. There is no traffic database to seize, subpoena, or leak.
privacy.s1_li5: Payments are optional and made in FLUX cryptocurrency on a public blockchain. We do not operate the blockchain, do not take card details, and never learn your name from a payment.
privacy.s1_li6: No trackers, no advertising SDKs, no analytics that identify you. The apps contain no third-party advertising or behavioural-tracking code.
privacy.s1_li7: We are not a data business. We have no user data to sell, and we do not sell data.
privacy.s2_p1: CumulusVPN ("we", "us") is a VPN service operated by the CumulusVPN team, built on the Flux decentralized cloud network (runonflux.io). VPN exit servers ("gateways") run as containerized apps on Flux datacenter nodes operated by independent, KYC-verified datacenter operators. We publish the service; the underlying compute is decentralized.
privacy.s2_p2:
For the purposes of the EU/UK General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), to the very limited extent
      any of the information below is "personal data", the CumulusVPN operating entity is the data
      controller. The lawful basis we rely on is our legitimate interest (Article 6(1)(f)) in providing a
      functioning, abuse-resistant VPN, and — for payments — performance of the service you request
      (Article 6(1)(b)). Because the service is designed to be anonymous, in most cases we hold no data
      that can be linked to you at all.
privacy.s3_intro: We want to be explicit, because most VPNs bury this. CumulusVPN does not collect, log, or store any of the following:
privacy.s3_li1: Your name, email address, phone number, or any account credentials — there are no accounts.
privacy.s3_li2: Your originating IP address in any persistent log.
privacy.s3_li3: The websites, domains, or services you connect to.
privacy.s3_li4: Your DNS queries (DNS is resolved for you through the tunnel and not recorded).
privacy.s3_li5: The contents, timing, or size of your traffic, in any persistent database.
privacy.s3_li6: A bandwidth history, session history, or connection-timestamp history tied to you.
privacy.s3_li7: Device advertising identifiers (IDFA / GAID) — we do not request or use them.
privacy.s3_li8: Location data from your device (GPS, precise or coarse). The country you connect through is your choice of server, not a reading of your device location.
privacy.s3_li9: Contacts, photos, files, microphone, camera, or any device sensor.
privacy.s3_p1:
We do not use third-party advertising networks, and we do not use analytics products that build a
      profile of you (such as ad-tech SDKs). The apps ship without behavioural trackers.
privacy.s3_p2: Our websites (cumulusvpn.com and vpn.cumulusvpn.com) use Cloudflare Web Analytics, a cookieless, aggregate measurement service: it sets no cookies, stores nothing on your device, and cannot identify or profile individual visitors. It tells us page counts, not people. The apps contain no analytics of any kind.
privacy.s4_h: 4. What is technically involved (and why it isn't a profile of you)
privacy.s4_intro:
A VPN cannot function without some data passing through it in real time. Here is all of it, and why
      none of it becomes a record about you.
privacy.s4_1_p: Your device generates a WireGuard key pair locally on first launch. The private key never leaves your device — not even to us. Only the public key is sent to a gateway so it can route your encrypted tunnel. A public key is a 32-byte random-looking value. It is not tied to your name, email, or hardware, and you can regenerate it at any time. It is a pseudonym, not an identity.
privacy.s4_2_h: 4.2 In-memory peer state on the gateway
privacy.s4_2_p: While you are connected, the gateway holds — in volatile memory (RAM) only — the minimum needed to route packets: your public key, the temporary internal tunnel IP assigned to it (e.g. 10.8.x.y), and a byte counter used to enforce your speed tier. This state is never written to disk, is not shipped to any central server, and is erased when the gateway process restarts or you disconnect. There is no historical log built from it.
privacy.s4_3_p: To carry your traffic, the gateway necessarily sees the IP address your connection arrives from, in the moment, at the network layer — this is how any internet server works. CumulusVPN does not log this address to any persistent store, and does not associate it with your browsing. When your session ends, it is not retained.
privacy.s4_4_p:
DNS look-ups are resolved on your behalf through the encrypted tunnel so your local network and ISP
      cannot see them. We do not keep DNS query logs.
privacy.s4_5_h: 4.5 How your app reaches the gateways
privacy.s4_5_p: Your app discovers gateways and reads their status by calling each gateway's control API directly. Those responses are signed with the gateway's ed25519 key and verified by the app, so tampering is detected even though the control channel itself is a plain connection — the integrity guarantee comes from the signature, not from a certificate. Your actual internet traffic is separate and is end-to-end encrypted by WireGuard between your device and the gateway.
privacy.s4_6_h: 4.6 What gateway operators can and cannot see
privacy.s4_6_p: Gateways are run on Flux datacenter nodes. Like any VPN exit, an operator observing their own server can see encrypted flow metadata in real time (that a peer is sending traffic), but not a persistent log we create, and not the tunnel contents. For users who do not want any single server to see both their IP and their destination, CumulusVPN offers an optional multi-hop mode: your traffic is routed through two gateways so that no single server sees both who you are and where you are going. Multi-hop is off by default and is described honestly in the app.
privacy.s5_h: 5. Payments (only if you upgrade to premium)
privacy.s5_p1: CumulusVPN has a free tier that requires no payment and no identifying information.
privacy.s5_p2: If you choose to upgrade to premium (higher speed), you pay in FLUX cryptocurrency on the Flux public blockchain. Important points:
privacy.s5_li1: Payment happens on the web or desktop, not inside the mobile apps. The mobile apps do not contain a purchase screen; they only display your current tier.
privacy.s5_li2: You send FLUX from your own wallet to our published payment address, including a short memo derived from your public key so the network can credit the right key. We do not collect card numbers, billing addresses, or names.
privacy.s5_li3: Blockchain transactions are public by nature. Anyone, including us, can see on the public ledger that a payment of a certain amount was made to our address with a given memo. That ledger is operated by the Flux blockchain, not by us. We do not link that transaction to your real-world identity, and the memo is derived one-way from your public key — it does not reveal your key or your traffic. Please understand that publishing a transaction to a public blockchain is inherently public; treat your wallet's privacy accordingly.
privacy.s5_li4:
Entitlement (whether a key is premium and until when) is computed deterministically from the public
        blockchain and keyed only to the public key. We store no separate customer billing record with your
        name in it, because we do not have your name.
privacy.s5_p3:
If you later use an in-app fiat payment option offered through Apple's App Store or Google Play
      (should we add one), that transaction is processed by Apple or Google under their own privacy
      policies; we receive only the confirmation needed to grant premium, not your payment card data.
privacy.s6_p1:
If you email us (for support or an abuse report), we receive whatever you put in that email — for
      example your email address and the text of your message. We use it only to answer you and keep it no
      longer than needed to resolve the matter. You are not required to identify yourself to use the
      service; contacting support is voluntary.
privacy.s6_p2: Optional, opt-in crash reports: if — and only if — you explicitly enable crash reporting, a crash report containing technical diagnostic information (such as the app version, device model, OS version, and a stack trace) may be sent to help us fix bugs. This is off by default, contains no browsing activity, and can be turned off at any time.
privacy.s7_p:
The CumulusVPN website (cumulusvpn.com) is a static site used for downloads, documentation, the
      payment page, and hosting our signed server directory. It does not use advertising cookies or
      third-party behavioural trackers. Any cookies used are strictly functional (for example remembering
      light/dark theme).
privacy.s8_p1: We do not sell, rent, or trade personal data — we have essentially none to sell.
privacy.s8_p2: Because we keep no activity logs and no user identities, we have nothing to hand over in response to a data request about a user's browsing. If we receive a legally valid law-enforcement or court request, we can only provide the information we actually hold, which does not include user identities, browsing history, DNS queries, or traffic contents, because those records do not exist. Where lawful and appropriate, we intend to publish a transparency report describing requests received.
privacy.s8_p3:
We do not transfer user profiles to advertisers, data brokers, or analytics vendors, because we do not
      build user profiles.
privacy.s9_p: CumulusVPN is a global network of gateways. When you connect, your traffic egresses from the country of the server you choose. Because we do not collect personal data into a central database, there is no cross-border transfer of a personal-data store to safeguard. The choice of exit country is yours and is made fresh each session.
privacy.s10_h: 10. Your rights (GDPR / UK GDPR / CCPA and similar)
privacy.s10_intro:
Depending on where you live, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, or port your personal
      data, to object to or restrict processing, and (in California) to know what is collected and to not be
      discriminated against for exercising these rights. Because the service is anonymous:
privacy.s10_li1: Access / portability: We generally hold no personal data linked to you to provide. If you emailed support, you can ask for a copy of that correspondence.
privacy.s10_li2: Deletion: There is nothing tied to your identity for us to delete from gateways; peer state is already ephemeral RAM-only and clears on disconnect/restart. You can delete your key on your device at any time, which fully severs the pseudonymous link.
privacy.s10_li3: Do-not-sell / do-not-share: We do not sell or share personal data, so there is nothing to opt out of.
privacy.s10_p: To exercise any right, email info@cumulusvpn.com. We may be unable to identify you in our (nonexistent) records precisely because the service is anonymous — that is the design, not an evasion. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority.
privacy.s11_p:
CumulusVPN is not directed to children under 13 (or the minimum age of digital consent in your
      country). We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. Because we collect no identifying
      data from anyone, we cannot determine a user's age; the service is intended for general audiences and
      is not marketed to children.
privacy.s12_p:
Traffic is encrypted end-to-end between your device and the gateway using the WireGuard® protocol
      (modern, audited cryptography). Your private key never leaves your device. Server-side peer state is
      kept in memory only. The app reaches each gateway's control API over a direct connection whose
      responses are ed25519-signed and verified by the app, so the authenticity of the server directory and
      gateway responses is protected by digital signatures rather than by a transport certificate.
privacy.s13_p: If we change this policy, we will update the "Last updated" date above and post the new version at cumulusvpn.com/privacy. Material changes will be highlighted. Because we keep no mailing list, the website is the authoritative source for the current policy.
privacy.trademark:
WireGuard is a registered trademark of Jason A. Donenfeld. CumulusVPN is an
      independent project and is not endorsed by or affiliated with the WireGuard project.
privacy.translation_note: This translation is provided for convenience; the English version at cumulusvpn.com/privacy is authoritative.
